rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The HPA api-frontend is maxed out at 3 replicas while CPU utilization is 135% of request (2.7× the 50% target), because the maxReplicas cap of 3 is too low to absorb sustained CPU load — the HPA cannot scale further to relieve the pressure.
contributingFactors: HPA maxReplicas is set to 3, which is too conservative for the observed sustained CPU demand
Current average CPU utilization across all 3 pods is 135% of request (target is 50%), meaning ~5-6 replicas would be needed to meet the SLO
Two pods (4m7rm, fgd6j) are each consuming 100m CPU — their full CPU limit — indicating they are CPU-throttled
CPU request of 50m per pod is very low, making the HPA react aggressively but unable to scale past the ceiling
The HPA ScalingLimited condition is True with reason 'TooManyReplicas', confirming it wants more pods but is blocked by maxReplicas
SuccessfulRescale event confirms HPA already scaled from 2→3 due to CPU above target and is now pinned at the ceiling
No previous remediation history exists for this deployment — this is a first-time configuration issue
One pod is scheduled on the control-plane node, indicating worker capacity pressure and potential scheduling constraints

kaResponse.analysis: The HPA api-frontend is maxed out at 3 replicas while CPU utilization is 135% of request (2.7× the 50% target), because the maxReplicas cap of 3 is too low to absorb sustained CPU load — the HPA cannot scale further to relieve the pressure.
